summary:
Claim: Bristle stiffness primarily depends on geometric dimensions such as diameter and length.  
Evidence: - Diameter directly influences stiffness due to greater cross-sectional area resisting bending. - Length contributes to structural rigidity when properly supported, though excessive length reduces effective stiffness.  
Answer: A
